items.title: USDA lists Arkansas SNAP waiver to restrict soda/candy purchases starting July 1, 2026
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Administration lists Arkansas as having an approved SNAP food-restriction waiver with a target implementation date of 07/01/26. The waiver restricts SNAP purchases of soda, fruit/vegetable drinks with less than 50% juice, energy drinks, unhealthy drinks, and candy.

items.title: CMS issues guidance on limits to federal Medicaid/CHIP funding for certain noncitizens starting Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS released guidance to states on implementing a statutory change that limits federal matching funds for full-scope Medicaid and CHIP for certain noncitizens beginning October 1, 2026. CMS notes federal matching funds remain available for treatment of emergency medical conditions and that the change does not apply to states’ options to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant women.

items.title: CMS interim final rule sets Medicaid “community engagement” requirement at 80 hours/month; comments due July 31, 2026
items.summary: CMS issued an interim final rule implementing a new Medicaid community engagement requirement for certain adults, generally requiring 80 hours per month of qualifying activities (work, education, job training, or community service) as a condition of eligibility. The rule is effective July 31, 2026, and states must generally implement the requirement no later than January 1, 2027.

items.title: USDA proposes reducing federal SNAP administrative cost share from 50% to 25% starting FY 2027; comments due Aug. 24, 2026
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Administration published a proposed rule to codify a reduction in the federal government’s share of SNAP state administrative costs from 50% to 25% beginning in fiscal year 2027. The notice says written comments must be received on or before August 24, 2026.

items.title: SSA posts 2026 SSI federal payment amounts: $994/month (individual) and $1,491/month (eligible couple)
items.summary: SSA’s 2026 SSI federal payment standards reflect the 2.8% COLA effective January 2026. The posted maximum monthly federal SSI amounts for 2026 are $994 for an eligible individual and $1,491 for an eligible individual with an eligible spouse (plus $498 for an essential person).

items.title: SSA confirms 2.8% COLA for 2026 affecting about 75 million Social Security and SSI recipients
items.summary: SSA says Social Security benefits and SSI payments for 75 million Americans increase by 2.8% in 2026. The increase begins with benefits payable in January 2026, and increased SSI payments begin with the December 31, 2025 payment.

items.title: USDA: WIC temporarily increases maximum monthly fluid milk allowances through Sept. 30, 2026
items.summary: USDA’s WIC policy memo says a temporary increase to the maximum monthly allowance (MMA) of fluid milk is in effect for FY 2026, including 16 quarts for some packages and up to 24 quarts for Food Package VII. USDA says the authority for the updated amounts terminates on September 30, 2026.

items.title: IRS posts EITC tables: maximum 2025 EITC is $8,046 (3+ qualifying children) and investment income limit is $11,950
items.summary: IRS EITC tables list the 2025 maximum credit amounts including $8,046 for filers with three or more qualifying children and $649 for filers with no qualifying children. The IRS also lists an investment income limit of $11,950 or less for tax year 2025 EITC eligibility.

items.title: FAFSA (2025–26) federal submission deadline is June 30, 2026; 2026–27 deadline is June 30, 2027
items.summary: USA.gov lists the federal FAFSA submission deadline for the 2025–26 school year as June 30, 2026. It also lists the federal FAFSA submission deadline for the 2026–27 school year as June 30, 2027.
